candidates.stmt:
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

Dear voter,

Thank you to everyone for your positive feedback so far during our election campaign.

It’s been great speaking to people from across the area, finding out what issues are important to you and what you want from a local councillor.

The last year has been fairly tough, but I have still been working hard to make a difference here in Craven Ward.   I love our area, which has been my home for the last 26 years. In that time I have been involved in many community groups – I have been a parent governor at Silsden Primary School, I am co-ordinator of Plastic Free Silsden and I am part of the local Emergency Planning Group (which helped provide support across the ward during last year’s first covid lockdown) – to name just a few. I hope you think that my work has made a difference.

In 2019, I was proud to be elected to Silsden Town Council with the highest number of votes of any of the elected councillors.

I co-founded Silsden Campaign for the Countryside three years ago and I will continue to stand up for local people in opposing inappropriate housing developments on our green spaces. I will be a strong, independently minded voice for our area - and I will hold Bradford Council to account.

Many people tell me that the Green Party is the only party that regularly delivers newsletters to homes across our area. I believe it is important that a local councillor keeps local residents up to date with what they are doing and keeps them informed about issues that affect our area.

The choice at this election is clear. Things can carry on as they are, or you can vote for someone who has been working hard all year to prove themselves worthy to be your next councillor.

Please use your vote to elect me to Bradford Council on May 6th.

Yours sincerely

Caroline Whitaker Green Party candidate for Craven Ward. P.S. The last election was close with only a few hundred votes between the top four candidates. With yo

See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

Janet gained an MA in French and German language and literature at St Andrews University, only to pursue a career as a folk singer for many years, after working as a secretary in London (whence came The Secretary's Song, most popular song of the year on Folk on 2, around 1986). Janet is now a community choir leader and a Trustee for Silsden's Youth and Children's Centre, The Hive. She lives with her partner Jim Woodland in Silsden.
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

Hi, my name is Ian, I am married with 2 grown up children. I’ve lived in Bradford for over 30 years. I’ve worked as a Procurement and Commercial Manager for 35 years, mostly within manufacturing companies in West Yorkshire.

I have not previously been involved in politics at a local or national level but felt the need to do something and offer people an alternative from the 2 main parties who both appear to be committed to imposing their politically correct agendas on the country whilst restricting the rights of ordinary citizens to voice their concerns.

My main political interests are in encouraging economic growth in the North through business incentives and investment in transport infrastructure and manufacturing. I have no time for net zero, gender debates or other minority issues that seem to have distracted our political class and mainstream media over the last few decades. My main concern is for the silent majority, those who have contributed to society, worked hard and done the right things in life but have seen their living standards dragged down by high taxes, unnecessary government spending and poor immigration control.

Outside of work, I enjoy Park Runs, dog walking and motorcycling.

Hoping you vote for Reform UK and help put a stop to the slow and unnecessary decline of the UK, its economy, its historic freedoms and its culture.

Introducing myself as your Labour Parliamentary Candidate.

While I know some of you will know me already, many of you will have seen us at our Campaign Launch, and plenty of you have spoken with us on the doorstep in Boroughbridge and Tadcaster and Wetherby, I simply wanted to formally introduce myself as your Parliamentary Candidate and let you know how you can get in touch.

- How can I get in touch? Please follow this page to see what we are up to across the constituency, send me a message at anytime about with any questions you might have, or send me an email at ben.pickles@gmx.co.uk.

I want to speak to as many people as possible so any messages at all are welcomed.

- Who am I? I'm a ‘proud council estate kid’ and Yorkshire lad, I grew up with a single mum and I was the first in my family to attend university. While at university in Leeds, Georgie and I had our first child as teenagers, and our second little one shortly after my final year.

I graduated in Politics and went straight into education.

I'm a schoolteacher and have been for nearly a decade, aiming to give back to the community that gave so much to me. I specialise in teaching English, Citizenship and A-Level Law.

- Can we win? Two recent polls for our constituency show we are ahead here. Some assume wrongly that it is a 'safe' Tory seat, but with the boundary changes, it is absolutely a seat we can win - I wouldn't be standing if I didn't think I could make real change.

There are so many other questions I could answer. Please do get in touch and make your voice heard. I look forward to speaking with you!

I hope the people of Bingley West will place their trust in me to continue working hard for our community—standing up for local residents, protecting vital services, and ensuring investment reaches the areas that need it most.

Bingley West has faced real challenges in recent years: pressure on local services, threats to our green belt and the rising cost of living impacting families and businesses alike.

Our green belt isn’t just land—it’s part of what makes Bingley West special. I will always stand up to protect it from inappropriate development, as I have for over a decade.

Families are being stretched to their limits. People are working hard and still struggling—that’s not right and it’s why supporting residents through the cost-of-living crisis is one of my top priorities.

I believe strong, experienced local leadership is essential to ensure our area gets its fair share and that decisions are made with residents, not for them.

Too often, decisions are made far away from the people they affect. I will make sure Bingley West has a strong voice at the table.

I have a proven record of delivering for our communities and am committed to building on that work fighting for better services, stronger neighbourhoods and a fairer future for all.

I am an experienced local politician who has previously served as a councillor on Bradford Council.

With a strong background in community and public service, I have also contributed beyond local government; I served within the NHS as a public governor representing the Bingley area, I spent seven years as a board member of Manningham Housing Association, seven years as the Vice Chair of Bradford District Credit Union and am a resident panel member for the Housing Ombudsman Service.

I’ve worked closely with local organisations and residents for years so I understand both the challenges we face and the practical solutions that can make a difference.

I actively support local organisations including credit unions and housing as

Local pub landlord Chris has worked in bars, pubs and restaurants for over 20 years. He understands the pressures on families and small businesses, and will work to support local pubs and farms, ensure                   safer streets for children and families and that our council tax is spent on delivering the frontline services we depend on including fixing                   potholes and keeping our communities clean.

I have lived in **Bingley** all my life and care deeply about our community. I went to **Beckfoot School**, I am a graphic designer, and I previously served as a **Bingley Town Councillor**.

My background is in communication and public-facing roles. One of the biggest problems with **Bradford Council** is poor communication with residents. Too often, people feel ignored while local services get worse and valued local places are neglected or sold off. I want to help change that.

I know how important it is for local people to have representatives who listen properly, keep people informed, and speak up clearly when something is not right. Good representation matters, especially when so many decisions are made away from the communities they affect.

I have supported residents in promoting biodiversity, taken part in community litter picks, campaigned to protect green spaces from inappropriate development, campaigned for better buses in rural areas, and was involved in the district-wide campaign to stop library closures, including **Bingley Library**.

For me, being a councillor is about listening to residents, understanding local concerns, and standing up for the area when decisions are being made. It is about making sure people are heard, not shut out.

**Bingley West** covers **Bingley** town centre, **Cottingley**, **Wilsden**, **Harden** and **Cullingworth**. Across the ward, people want better services, planning decisions they can trust, protected green spaces, and local places that are cared for properly.

My priorities are:

* Protect green spaces
* Oppose the short-term sell-off of community assets
* Push for better services
* Improve communication from **Bradford Council**
* Make sure residents are listened to and kept informed

I am proud to be standing alongside **Cath Bacon** and **Rachael Drucquer** in **Bingley West**.

Because the ward boundaries have changed, this year’s council election is an all-out election. That means voters in **Bingley West** can
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

It is an honour and a privilege to be selected as Labour’s Bradford Council Candidate in Ilkley, my hometown.

The average age of a councillor is 61 and the majority are retired, so I hope to offer something new and engaging. I was crushed by our election defeat in December 2019, but I believe we can build back our support, starting at a local level. Labour is, and must be, the party of the future, not the past. I joined the Labour Party in 2015, despite being from a Conservative family, because I believe it is the only vehicle to achieve social justice and change in Britain.

I was born, raised, and educated in Ilkley before going to Manchester Metropolitan University and latterly the University of Huddersfield. I am currently in my final year of a drama degree and due to finish in May. I have worked in several hospitality venues in the town and for last seven years I have worked at the Ilkley based Virtual College. In addition to this I also run a micro part time catering business and a voluntary dog walking service.

I have been involved in the Ilkley community all my life through my membership of Upstagers both as a drama teacher and a performer, and I have become Ilkley’s resident pantomime villain! I am passionate about all Ilkley has to offer, as we are blessed with such a vibrant arts scene in the town. Sadly, due to the pandemic, all drama teaching has been put on hold, but I am currently in the process of planning some form of online drama coaching. I had also previously been doing online storytelling for my Upstagers students.

If elected I will:
Speak up for and promote Ilkley’s many fantastic independent businesses.
Ilkley is fortunate to have so many amazing local businesses, all of which make up the backbone of the local economy. As we begin our recovery from Covid-19 it is vital we all do our bit to ensure their futures.

Support schemes to improve the environment in Ilkley and beyond.

I am delighted that the River Wharfe in Ilkley has

Hi, I’m Paul Shkurka, proud Bradfordian, proud Yorkshireman and proud to be British. Born in Yorkshire, I was raised, educated and have lived in the Bradford district all my life, with the lovely village of Wilsden being home for the past 25 years.  Now retired, I spent 30+ years in varied management roles, in operations & distribution, with the last three spent working in the fraud department of a major bank.   On retirement I became politically active after discovering the SDP.  Their New Declaration document with its key slogan – Family, Community, Nation, sums up the SDP Communitarian ethos that drew me into the party. Like many I despair at the decline we are seeing, in our society, in our politics, in our prosperity and quality of life, with our public services, industries and national infrastructure in serious decline. A housing market our young are unable to access, low wage economy, ever growing inequalities, and the growing threat to traditional British values and culture, being common concerns many share.  

I can sit back and complain, or I can in some small way try to make a difference. I chose the latter, to stand as a candidate representing a communitarian party that truly has your and our Nations interests at heart.

Although many voters, rightly, have concerns about the decline and poor delivery of vital national services that they care about and that may affect them personally, and which are often local in nature, these can only be fully addressed at a national level. Hence sorting these issues for Shipley resident’s means sorting them nationally, whether that’s NHS waiting lists, lack of GP and dentist access, cost of living difficulties or housing shortages, to name but a few.      The same old politics practiced through terms of successive Tory and Labour governments over the past decades have resulted in a steady national decline, which is plain for all to see. As many of us, particularly the less well off and vulnerable in our society are expe

About Me  I am from Bradford . I was raised on the HolmeWood estate until I was 16, then relocated to Bradford Moor for six years before moving to Ravenscliffe in 2011. As well as being pro-actively involved in the community, I am a governor at a local school. 

 I am standing up for the  people of Bradford south who have been let down by the Westminster elite who have inflicted crushing austerity against local services, cuts to benefits effecting thousands of local residents. 

If elected I will Fight for better funding for yorkshire Better transport links  Fight crime with more police on the front line  Invest in green tech to protect out environment Improve access to health social and dental care

"You know, sometimes you just have to do what you think is the right thing. I've been punished by my own party for speaking out on controversial issues, Palestine and what the Isrealis are doing and many people know about these things, but it's actually quite tough when you're willing to speak out about them. I've decided that I want to carry on representing you as I did before and being able to speak out on controversial issues, free of any party control. So I am standing as an independent candidate in this general election to be your MP, your independent MP so I can continue to fight on strong issues, issues that are controversial, but also for Bradford, because I want to be able to vote on every single issue in the House of Commons on what is right for Bradford. And so I want to be your MP, here not anywhere else, here in Bradford East, where I live, where my family is, where my future is and where I want to be part of your future."

Source: http://www.davidward.org.uk/why_i_am_standing_as_an_independant

Iarlaithe Ward, pronounced ear-lah, is an 18 year old Green Party member & candidate from Bradford. He has been a supporter since 2022, and an official member since 2025. Iarlaithe is a college student, leftist, and activist who has been involved with various activism groups & movements.

Standing up for everyday people is the only way forward. I believe in building a fairer, cleaner, and more compassionate Bradford — a place where community, creativity, and sustainability come together to shape our future. The Green Party’s values reflect my own belief that politics should serve people and the planet, not profit or power.

As someone who has lived, worked, and volunteered across Bradford, I’ve seen both the challenges our city faces and the outstanding people who make the city tick. I’ve been involved with the Green Party through campaigning and leafleting, and as an organiser for the Bradford Young Greens. These experiences have shown me how grassroots action can bring real change — from engaging new voters to supporting local campaigns that give residents a voice in decisions that affect their lives.

Through my background in the creative sector I have been blessed with unique perspectives. Producing and directing a documentary about artists in West Yorkshire taught me how important culture and storytelling are in building identity and community pride. Through volunteering with organisations like Pictureville Cinema and Fashion Fiesta CIC, I’ve supported local projects that bring people together and promote inclusion and opportunity.

As an elected representative, I will focus on improving local wellbeing, supporting sustainable transport, and protecting our green spaces. I want to contribute to making Bradford a city where every resident — regardless of background — can thrive in a safe, affordable, and environmentally responsible community. I am committed to listening, learning, and working collaboratively with residents, campaigners, and fellow councillors to make that happen.

Standing for the Green Party isn’t just about winning an election for me — it’s about being part of a movement that puts integrity, care, and sustainability at the heart of everything we do. I am ready to represent the people of Bradford with honesty, energy, and a clear

Background

Brendan is married to Tricia and they have a seven-year-old son and one-year-old daughter. Brendan works in Greengates as a graphic designer. Originally from Halifax, he has worked in Bradford for over twenty years. He started his career working at the Victoria Hall in Saltaire before moving to the Horsfall Stadium, home to Bradford Park Avenue, before becoming a graphic designer.

Experience

Brendan is a dedicated and active campaigner and Councillor for Eccleshill Ward. He is a member of the Eccleshill Village Steering Group and Friends of Chapel Street Cemetery. He has worked alongside volunteers across Eccleshill Ward to help organise a variety of events and activities including neighbourhood clean-ups, tree and bulb planting, as well as the annual Village Fair and Christmas celebrations.

Brendan has worked alongside the Lib Dem team to protect local services, campaigned against the destruction of our green spaces and argued for greater investment in local infrastructure. Working with other Liberal Democrat councillors across Bradford, Brendan is also campaigning for greater investment in Policing, along with Council Warden and enforcement services so that we can tackle those who break the rules, damage our local environment and have had it too easy for too long.

Over five years as a councillor Brendan has successfully presented Council motions to increase enforcement action against the misuse of fireworks, to recognise and improve the support for carers across the district as well as arguing for greater support for children and the services they rely on. He has campaigned to get private landlords properly regulated and fought to protect the library, leisure and museum services. Brendan also served as the Deputy Chair of the Bradford East Area Committee. He has allocated over £20,000 to community groups and good causes across Eccleshill Ward in the past two years.

Brendan has been a strong voice for Eccleshill Ward. He has led the fig

Having lived in Bradford my entire life I am keen to secure the best for Bradford. Therefore, I believe that the Green Party, with the policies they stand for, can best represent and further Bradford and the Great Horton community.
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

Matt has been a councillor on Bradford Council since May 2021 - the first Green Party councillor in Bradford South. He has a track record of working hard for the local community. Matt has demanded more action on fly-tipping, worked with the police to deal with anti-social behaviour and has helped residents with many different sorts of issues. He has worked with local groups to oppose unsuitable developments and called for action to redevelop empty brownfield sites. He has been vocal calling for action to speed up work to reopen Bradford Inter-change Bus Station. When not campaigning in the local community, he works as a self-employed graphic designer and can usually be found walking his dogs.

See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

I have lived in Bradford almost all of my life. I work in central Bradford for an asylum seekers charity. I have been employed in community regeneration projects in Bradford and Keighley for over 20 years. I believe that Bradford is a wonderful and vibrant city with huge potential to be an amazing place to live, work and play; however we need strong leadership to raise the profile of the city regionally and nationally. The Green Party representation nationally needs to be broadened to change people's lives for the better; better housing, better health services and better green spaces.
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

Jeanette lives in Bradford East with her husband Andrew. She currently works in training and development in the public sector. 

Jeanette has a first class honours degree in Social Psychology. Jeanette is a Trustee of a Youth Centre on Thorpe Edge and is part of a group organising fund-raising events to support the Marie Curie Hospice in Bradford.   

Jeanette has a strong record of community action. She is a Councillor representing Idle and Thackley ward. 

Jeanette is a passionate advocate for ensuring that every child can get on in life. Over the past few years, she has volunteered at a Summer Camp for children making the transition from primary to secondary school. Jeanette has raised almost a quarter of a million pounds to ensure the project continues for the next three years. 

Jeanette is currently campaigning to see the remaining green spaces around Eccleshill, Esholt, Thackley and Apperley Bridge owned by the Council and Yorkshire Water turned into a Country Park, ensuring the future of our woodlands for the benefit of people and the environment.

See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

Ros has lived in Ben Rhydding for over 15 years with her husband and their children who attend the local secondary school. She has worked as a headteacher, teacher and academic and is a runner, cyclist and member of her local parish church supporting children’s work and Fairtrade.

Ros first got involved in local politics 10 years ago to represent the voices of residents not heard by Ilkley’s District and Town Councillors and MP. She was elected on to Ilkley Town Council and served for four years working hard as an unpaid councillor and Deputy Mayor. This gave Ros experience in getting things done for residents.

Ros says “Like many residents, I was shocked and outraged at the closing of Ilkley’s tip and oppose this. It is unfair and short-sighted economically and environmentally. Green Party councillors on Bradford Council also voted against this. Together with the Bradford Green councillors, I will continue to push for more action to tackle river pollution. I also share residents’ concerns about the lack of properly affordable housing in Ilkley and the need to resist the loss of Ilkley’s green belt within the Bradford District Local Plan.”

Ros also engages with the challenges and opportunities of Ilkley being a popular destination and seeks to alleviate areas of hidden need in our community and support local business sustainability. She is keen to listen to the concerns and ideas of all residents, including children and young people and has established good working relationships with a range of individuals, community groups and stakeholders.

Ros wants to continue to serve Ilkley’s residents well through holding both Bradford Council and Ilkley’s MP to account for decisions made at District and national level that impact Ilkley.

Ros has shown herself to be a strong, independently minded voice who can be counted on to stand up for local people, local services and our precious environment.

NB In addition
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

I am Andrew Judson, Your Reform UK Parliamentary Candidate For Keighley and Ilkley.

A little about me.

I was born in Oakworth, attending Oakworth Primary through to Bronte Middle and lastly Oakbank school, Leaving school I worked in arboriculture (tree work), I then went on to run a successful business in Keighley in the building industry and now work alongside my partner, running our family business in the garden and equine sector, I also still utilise my knowledge in remedial treatment of property, being a consultant for home buyers.

I’m passionate about the care of animals, and I have 5 rescue horses, 3 rescue dogs, and 3 rescue house cats.

I have and still reside and work in the town of Keighley and surrounding areas,

I am currently an active Councillor on Keighley Town Council for the Fell Lane and Westburn Ward, I am a very proactive councillor rather than reactive.

I am also heavily involved in the following: I take these roles very seriously and with great responsibility.

* Chair of the Neighbourhood Plan Development Group.

Which is steering planning policy for the area. In which I orchestrated a meeting between the group and SELRAP, the organization that is responsible for campaigning for the re-opening of the Colne to Skipton Rail line, the presentation highlighted the enormous benefits on reopening this line for our region as well as the entire north of England and I whole heartedly support their endeavours.

*Member of the Planning committee.

*Public spaces and assets committee.

*Representative on the Yorkshire Local Council Association.

*Chair of Governors of 2 primary schools in the area.

*Council appointed to Beckfoot Nessfield local school committee.

As these active and varied roles, I see the issues the local area and its people face in these testing times. I am very aware for a need to build more homes, but at what cost to those already residing in the area?

We are witnessing too many large scale developments in areas such as Silsden

PEOPLE FIRST
SAFER STREETS
YOUTH CENTRES
CLAMP DOWN ON DRUGD

Hello, I am Councillor Safina Kauser, your local councillor for Manningham Ward, proud to represent our community and standing for re-election in May 2026 to continue the work we have already started together.

I was born and raised in Manningham, educated locally, and now live and raise my family here. I am also a qualified lecturer with over 15 years’ experience in education.

Since being elected in May 2023, I have focused on the issues that matter most to residents, including SEND, fly-tipping, potholes, blocked gullies, street lighting and unadopted roads, taking action through days of action, working with council teams and key stakeholders, delivering real results.

Residents have raised concerns about safety, clean streets, opportunities for young people and pressure on local services. I have acted on these issues, working to protect the community, improve local environments, support youth initiatives and make sure residents’ voices are heard in council decisions.

Alongside this, I have protected vital services such as Manningham Sports Centre and Midland Road tip, remained accessible through surgeries, ward walks, phone and email, and continue to support residents behind the scenes with housing, school appeals and access to services, remaining committed to delivering on the pledges I made to Manningham.

I am asking for your support in the May 2026 election so I can continue serving our community and delivering results for Manningham.

My name is Fatima Ali and I was born and raised in Manningham. I attended Iqra Academy and grew up in the heart of this community, where neighbours looked out for one another and where I developed a strong sense of pride in our area.

Inspired by my father’s well-known community work, I have been involved in local grassroots campaigning from a young age. I am currently studying Applied Science and hope to work in healthcare, driven by a passion for helping others.

I am standing because I care deeply about our community and the people who live here. Having grown up in Manningham, I understand the challenges local families face and the importance of having someone who listens and takes action.

I am committed to being approachable, hardworking and present, and I hope to earn your support.

After speaking with many residents and local businesses across Manningham, I’ve been encouraged to stand in the local elections on 7 May 2026.

I’m proud to be selected as a Labour candidate for Manningham Ward.

Having lived in Manningham most of my life, I understand our community and the challenges we face. I have spent decades working in youth and community development across Bradford, and I am a local businessman, charity director and General Secretary of a Masjid in Frizinghall.

I know residents are concerned about crime, fly-tipping, opportunities for young people and pressure on local services. If elected, I will work to make our streets safer, improve cleanliness, support young people and ensure Manningham’s voice is heard.

This election is about our community and our future. Together, we can build a safer, cleaner, stronger Manningham.

I would be honoured to have your support.

Dear residents of Queensbury WARD, Most of you who are cricket enthusiasts may already know me, but for those who don’t, here’s a quick intro.
I grew up and studied in Germany, and I speak German and English alongside my mother tongue. By profession, I’m an IT engineer, working hands-on to keep systems running smoothly and solving problems every day.
Outside of work, my real passion has always been community building through sport. I’ve been actively involved in launching and promoting grassroots cricket initiatives in Germany and here in the UK, helping bring people together and creating opportunities for young people.
I also represent my colleagues as a Workers’ Council Representative at a globally recognised British media company, making sure their voices are heard and supported.
My efforts have been recognised with the Connecting Communities Award (Buckinghamshire, 2024) and the Official of the Year Award (Yorkshire Cricket Board, 2025) for my contribution to Sports.
https://yorkshirecricketfoundation.com/2025/11/24/cricket-collective-winners-awarded/

Standing as a councillor candidate is a natural next step for me. It will give me the chance to do even more, especially for young people in Queensbury, by creating opportunities, supporting new projects, and strengthening our community.
I understand the commitment this role requires, and I’m fully prepared to give my time and energy. I’ll also be working behind the scenes to support the Women’s Cricket World Cup 2026 in Headingley and across the UK, so you might spot me around on match days!
You have three votes this year, so I would be grateful if you would consider giving me one of them and a chance to represent you. I will do my best to earn your trust and make a real difference.
I’m looking forward to representing our community and helping build a better future for our youth
because for me, it will always be #CommunityFirst.

URL : https://tinyurl.com/dj6znwjv

See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

Kevin and his family have lived in Shipley for 25 years and he has represented the Shipley ward on Bradford Council for the Green Party since 2004. In addition to his wide-ranging duties as an elected councillor, he teaches Politics and History at a sixth form college in Leeds.

Originally from Chesterfield, Chris Turner studied Software Engineering in Leeds in the mid-1980s. He also subsequently studied Psychology and Counselling. Chris moved to Burley-in-Wharfedale in 2000 and works for a global electronics company in Saltaire. He has 2 daughters at secondary school in Ilkley and has been campaigning against local green-belt declassification and development.
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

I have been a councillor for Wyke for two years.

I started my career working at a local charity, securing funding and developing projects that help people get on and get up in life – work that helped people to
find good jobs, to get involved in their community, and helped tackle social isolation.

Working with my brilliant Labour colleagues, I've dealt with problem fly-tipping, dangerous driving, nuisance fireworks, and fought for action on anti-social behaviour and crime.

I believe that every person deserves access to good opportunities in life, and that everyone in Wyke ward should be able to influence the decisions that affect where they live and work.

A good local councillor should be active and visible all year round. Let's work together to make our community better for everyone.
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

I'll keep it simple - I am looking to make real change at the council by focussing on local services and issues. I was born in Bradford and live in Bradford so have been as frustrated as everyone else watching our council chase vanity projects whilst every day services are cut to the bone. All whilst raising our council tax even higher.

I want to see practical changes to tackle fly tipping, improve road maintenance, reduce anti social behaviour and improve our waste / recycling services.

I want to see less funding for non-essential projects - we cannot be asking residents to pay more council tax while greenlighting non-essential projects.
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.
